properties.tree_description: The subject tree is a mature oak located within close proximity to adjacent residential structures. At the base of the trunk, multiple fungal fruiting bodies are present, including decayed conks and active fungal growth emerging from the root flare. Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Positioned immediately adjacent to a retaining wall, a restricted, deformed structural root plate condition is observed, indicating constrained root development. Noticeable canopy thinning is present with areas of radial dieback and sporadic sections of reduced or absent leaf-out. Basal sprouting is present at the base, indicating physiological stress. These observed conditions are cumulative and reflect an overall decline in vigor and structural integrity.
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:River Birch Tree Size (DBH):13Tree location:See site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Exhibits an approximate lean of 25 degrees (measured at 24.7 degrees) toward the adjacent property, confirmed using a clinometer. The canopy is offset from the basal area by approximately 20 feet. The canopy primarily consists of a co-dominant, overextended leader, likely resulting from a prior topping or break event.

properties.tree_location: front mid lot along active gravel alley
properties.tree_description:
Mature oak tree approximately 30–32 inches DBH exhibiting multiple structural and health concerns. The canopy shows notable lower canopy dieback and reduced vigor . The main stem contains visible cavities and internal decay along a major (highlighted in red), indicating compromised structural integrity . Evidence of previous limb failure and dropped branches is present, confirming ongoing structural decline . The tree also shows loss of a major limb resulting in an unbalanced canopy, increasing the likelihood of further failure . Vines are present throughout the canopy, adding additional stress and obscuring defects. Removal recommended due to extensive structural defects, canopy imbalance, and elevated risk of limb or whole-tree failure.Reason for Removal:Dying tree
		
		
			Tree number:1Species:PecanTree Size (DBH):16Tree location:front mid lot along active gravel alleyDescription of Tree:Mature pecan tree located along a gravel alley, with an estimated diameter of 14–18 inches DBH. The tree exhibits significant basal decay, including an open cavity and visible internal wood deterioration at the root flare. The base shows structural weakening and loss of sound wood, indicating compromised stability. The tree is positioned directly adjacent to an actively used alley/drive area, increasing risk exposure to vehicles and pedestrians. Removal recommended due to compromised base structure and elevated risk of failure in a high-use area.Reason for Removal:Dying tree
